data.executiveSummary: The qa-login service experienced a high error rate due to Redis connection timeouts, significantly impacting user authentication processes.

rootCause.title: Redis Connection Timeout
rootCause.description: The `qa-login` service encountered a session store retrieval failure due to connection timeouts with Redis, with a repeated timeout period of 5000ms, leading to service disruptions.

evidenceChain: Prometheus metrics show a service error rate of approximately 30%.
Loki logs indicate repeated 'LoginException' errors related to 'Session store retrieval failure. Redis connection timeout after 5000ms.'

timeline.event: LoginException in qa-login: Session store retrieval failure. Redis connection timeout after 5000ms.
LoginException in qa-login: Session store retrieval failure. Redis connection timeout after 5000ms.

data.impact: Users were unable to log in due to a high rate of Redis connection timeouts, affecting all authentication processes handled by the `qa-login` service.

similarIncident.title: Redis Connection Timeout Spikes
similarIncident.resolution: Resolution: Restarted redis-primary-3 instance due to orphaned connections.

recommendedActions: Restart the affected Redis instance to clear possible orphaned connections.
Investigate Redis connection pool configurations to ensure they are optimized to prevent timeouts.
Implement monitoring and alerts for Redis connection metrics to capture and address similar issues proactively.
